title: ChIP-SICAP
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bqf6c","text":"Chromatin immunoprecipitation combined with mass spectrometry (ChIP-MS) suffers from unexpected proteins that bind to chromatin during the cell lysis and immunoprecipitation (IP). The so-called hitchhikers of chromatin are hardly washed away by gentle washing steps applied to a normal IP protocol. We employed DNA end-labeling by Terminal Transferase (TdT) to purify proteins specifically bound to chromatin. Using this double purification strategy, chromatin fragments are released from IP beads, and re-captured by streptavidin beads. Thus, we can apply stringent conditions to remove the hitchhikers of chromatin, and to identify authentic proteins co-localized with the protein of interest. We call this protocol Selective Isolation of Chromatin-Associated Proteins (SICAP). So far, ChIP-SICAP was successfully applied to different cell lines such mouse ES cells, mouse fibroblast cells, HEK 293T, HeLa, U2OS as well as mouse and human tissues. Here, we share the most updated version of our protocol. If you have any questions please feel free to email us.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"50r6j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fp5sr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bgdl1","text":"This protocol has 4 parts. Part #1 and #2 are essentially ChIP. If you already have a optimised ChIP protocol you may also use your protocol. Part #1 has two alternative approaches for crosslinking the cells by formaldehyde. So you may choose either approach A or B. Approach A is detaching the cells, and then counting, fixing, and freezing them. The approach B is crosslinking the cells in the plate, and then harvesting and freezing them. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6e2fh","text":"There are 2 appendices in this protocol. Appendix #1 is a protocol for retrieving DNA to use it for qPCR or sequencing. Appendix #2 is a protocol for modification of streptavidin beads to avoid streptavidin contamination in mass spectrometry. Before you start, please use Appendix #2 to modify your streptavidin beads. You may also do this during Part #2: ChIP (Step 47).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"atb8f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"31koc","text":"For each replicate, you may need 4-24 million cells. The negative control for this assay is a no-antibody control, or normal IgG. Non-biotinylated control is not necessary, however, you may include a non-biotinylated control to demonstrate the identified proteins are chromatin-binders. The best negative control is a knock-out control to assess the specificity of the antibody. Also in order to compare with the negative control, you don’t need SILAC or other types of peptide labelling. However, labeling helps when you compare two or more cellular conditions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"29dh1","text":"Please work with formaldehyde and Sodium borohydride in a fume hood. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Detach the cells by Accutase. Accutase doesn’t lyse cells, so it is more suitable for single cell preparation. After detaching the cells, make sure that they are single cell by pipetting in the medium.
Count the cells.
Resuspend the cells in formaldehyde 1.5% in PBS. Roughly for every 10 million mammalian cells, add 10ml formaldehyde 1.5%. Pipette up and down to disrupt the cell pellet completely.
Rotate occasionally, and keep it 15min at room temperature.
Add Glycine 1M to stop the crosslinking, so the final concentration will be 130mM (1.5ml Glycine for every 10ml of formaldehyde).
Rotate occasionally 5min at room temperature.
Spin the cells at 2000RPM, 2min. Discard the supernatant (SN).
Resuspend the cells with PBS-T, equal volume to the formaldehyde volume that you'd used in step 4
Spin the cells at 2000RPM, 2min, and discard the SN.
Resuspend the cells in some PBS-T
Inside a fume hood, remove the medium, and pour 10ml of formaldehyde 1.5% in a 10cm dish.
·If you estimate there are more than 10 million cells, you may pour 20ml of formaldehyde
Wait 15min, and rotate the plate gently.
Add Glycine to the final concentration of 130mM
Wait 5min, and rotate the plate gently.
In the meantime, mix LB3 with 1x Complete protease inhibitor. For each 10cm plate you may need 10ml of LB3
Discard the liquid, and pour 10ml of PBS
Rotate the plate gently, and gently discard the liquid
Repeat the last two steps once again.
Pour 3ml of LB3+Complete on the plate.
·LB3 allows to collect the cells more efficiently. While, using PBS you will lose quite some cells.
10.Scrap the cells with LB3, and collect them in one side of the plate. Then collect them and pour them in a Falcone tube (15 or 50ml depending on the volume)
11.Repeat the last two steps, twice.
12.Spin the cells at 2000RPM for 2 min, and discard the SN
13.Resuspend the cells in LB3+Complete
14.Repeat the last two steps once again.
15.To measure the protein concentration, take 5ul of the cells, pour it in a PCR tube and add 1ul of Benzonase
16.Incubate at 37°C for 30min
17.Add 30ul of SDS wash buffer, and incubate at 95°C for 5 min
18.Measure the protein concentration by a BCA kit

·The standards of the BCA should be treated the same. Namely, mix 5ul of the standards with 30ul of the SDS wash buffer, and incubate them at 95°C for 5min. Then 25ul of each sample/standard should be loaded on a 96w plate to measure the protein concentrations by the BCA reagents. A 2mg/ml standard and blank standard would be enough to compare the protein concentrations of the samples.
19.Calculate the protein concentrationsAdjust the protein concentration with LB3+compele, aliquot the samples
Count the cells, and Aliqoute them. e.g. every 24 millions in a 15-ml tube Use polypropylen (soft) tubes. Do not use polystyrene (rigid) tubes, as a lot of cells bind to the walls.
Spin the cells at 2000 RPM 2min, and discard the SN. Freeze the cells in liquid nitrogen Pause point : You may freeze the cells in -80°C for months.
Wait 15min, and rotate the plate gently.
Add Glycine to the final concentration of 130mM
Rotate the plate gently, and wait 5min.
Discard the liquid, gently pour 10ml of PBS on the cells, rotate, and discard it
Repeat the last step once again.

Pour 3ml of PBS-T (Tween 0.1%) on the plate. Triton allows to collect the cells more efficiently. While, using PBS you will lose quite some cells. Adding Complete protease inhibitor is optional. Apparantely, endogenous proteases are inactivated by formaldehyde.
Scrap the cells with a cell lifter, and collect them in one side of the plate. Then transfer them by a pipet to a tube (e.g. 15ml or 50ml depending on the number of the plates)
Repeat the last two steps, once again for each plate.
Spin the cells at 2000RPM for 2 min, and discard the supernatant
Resuspend the cells in PBS-T, and aliqoute the them if you wish
Spin the cells at 2000RPM 2 min, and discard the supernatant
Freeze the cells in liquid nitrogen, and keep them in -80 ºC Pause point : You may freeze the cells in -80°C for months.
Inside a fume hood, remove the medium, and pour 10ml of formaldehyde 1.5% in a 10cm dish.
Spin the cells at 200g for 5min, and remove the medium.
1-Resuspend
million cells in 1ml of LB3+Complete and vortex, and divide it into 2x 1.5ml tubes specifically designed for Bioruptor pico
0pt;line-height:150%;font-family:"Arial",sans-serif'>Do not pour more than 12million cells in one tube for sonication.
2-Keep on ice for 5min
3-Spin 2000g, 2min
4-Discard the supernatant.
5-Resuspend the pellet in 500ul of LB3+ Complete, and repeat the steps 2 to 4, once again.
6-Resuspend each tube in 300ul of LB3 buffer.

7-Sonicating conditions depends on the cell lines. Bioruptor Pico is faster, however, Covaris is more precise and reproducible. You may start with Bioruptor Pico, and apply 7 cycles ON and OFF (30’’-30’’).
·If you used approach #2 for crosslinking, please thaw the cell lysates, and transfer 300ul to the sonication tubes. This should be about 1mg proteins as one replicate.
·Adding SDS to the final concentration of 0.1% improves the efficiency of sonication. However, some antibodies are sensitive to it.
8-After the sonication spin the sonication tubes 10min at 12000g
9-Collect the supernatant, and transfer it into a new 2ml epi-tube.

0pt;line-height:150%;font-family:"Arial",sans-serif'>You may take 5ul of the samples, to check the sheared chromatin. Boil and clean up DNA using QIAgen PCR purification kit. Run the DNA on an agarose gel. The smear should be <1kb. Unlike ChIP-seq that needs short fragments, ChIP-SICAP works with larger fragments. You may optimize the sonication. Alternatively, you may add a restriction enzyme in step 24 to cut the chromatin to smaller fragments.
Resuspend the cells in 0.9ml Tris-Cl 10mM. Then add 100ul Tritone X100 10%(v/v). Leave the cells on ice for 5 min Optional: You may also add Complete protease inhibitor.
Spin the cells 2min at 2000RPM. Discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the cells in 1ml LB3 buffer. Spin the cells 2min at 2000RPM. Discard the supernatant.
Repeat Step 31, twice again.

Resuspend the cells in LB3 buffer. Resuspend every 8 million cells in 250ul LB3 buffer, and-so-forth. One 10cm dish of confluent mES cells is about 20 million cells. Therefore, you may resuspend the cells in 750ul LB3 buffer.

Sonicating conditions depends on the cell lines. For mES cells using Bioruptor Pico, you may apply 7 cycles ON and OFF (30’’-30’’). Bioruptor Pico is faster, however, Covaris is more precise and reproducible. Adding SDS to the final concentration of 0.1% improves the efficiency of sonication. However, some antibodies are sensitive to it. You may add 0.1% sodium deoxycholate or 0.1% Sarkosyl. Too many cycles of sonication blast the proteins from chromatin.
After the sonication spin the sonication tubes 10min at 12000g
Collect the supernatant, and transfer it into a 2ml epi-tube or a 15ml tube. You may pool the sheared chromatin obtained from one replicate. LoBind tubes slightly improve the results.

Measure the protein concentration using a BCA protein assay kit. Briefly, take 5-10ul of the sheared chromatin, and pour it into a PCR tube. Add 25ul of PBS-SDS 1%. Do the same with your standard controls, and the negative control. Heat the samples at 95 ºC for 5min. Add 0.5ul of Benzonase, and gently vortex. Take 25ul of each sample, and pour it in a 96-w plate. Follow the kit manual to measure the protein concentration.
Aliqoute the resuspended cells into Biorupotor Pico 1.5ml special tubes. Pour every 250ul of the resuspended cells in a Biorupotor Pico 1.5ml special tube.

Pour up to 1 mg protein (chromatin) in a 2-ml epi-tube as one replicate. If you are using SILAC to compare two/three cellular states using one antibody you may mix the samples at this step. However, if you want to use SILAC to compare your antibody with normal IgG or no-antibody control, please mix the samples in step
Add Triton X100 from a 10% (v/v) stock to make the final conc. 1% Pause point : You may freeze the samples in -80°C for weeks.

Put the tubes on the magnet, after 1min remove the liquid, and take the tubes off the magnet. Immidiately pour 1000ul of Tris-HCl 10mM pH 7.5 (without EDTA) on the beads. Process the tubes one by one. Never allow the beads to dry. Here the idea is to remove the inhibitors of the enzymatic reactions (e.g. EDTA). Stringent washes come later.

Resuspend the beads by rotating end to end, and transfer them to a new 2ml epi-tube. 2ml epi-tube is necessary because agitation is more efficient, and beads do not precipitate in the subsequent steps
Keep 1%, as the input control for ChIP-qPCR/seq
Add proper amounts of your antibody to the samples. Depending on the antibody you may need to add between 2-10 ug or 1:50 – 1:100 v/v.
Agitate at 800RPM overnight in a Thermomixer (eppendorf) at 4°C or cold room
After the overnight incubation, spin the samples at 12000g for 10min at 4°C

Collect 95% of the liquid and transfer it into a new 2-ml epi-tube You may not see the pellet easily, however, sometimes there is a transparent precipitation A 2-ml epi-tube is necessary for efficient agitation in the next steps.
Adjust the volume to 1ml by adding IP buffer.
For each tube, you will need to prepare 40ul of ProteinA or ProteinG Dynabeads (depending on the antibody). Wash the magnetic beads once with IP buffer. More washing is not necessary.
Add 40ul magnetic Dynabeads to each tube
Rotate the samples 2-4 hours at 4°C (cold room) on a rotating wheel. Alternatively, you may rotate the samples overnight at 4°C.
Resuspend the beads in 200ul of NEB Cutsmart buffer 1x. Don’t pipet the beads. Gently swirl the tube to resuspend the beads.

Optional : If you wish to improve the efficiency of DNA end-labeling you may perform the folloing step. Although TdT adds nucleotides to 3’- and 5’-overhangs and blunt ends, Klenow 3’exo- adds biotinylated dATP to only 5’overhangs or blunt ends. Combination of these two enzymes improves DNA end-labelling about 10-20%. In addition, T4 PNK removes 3'-phosphates, and prepares the 3'-OH ends for TdT and Klenow. Prepare the following mixture for each tube. You may prepare a master mix for all the tubes: Cutsmart buffer 10x 10 ul Klenow 3’exo- 2 ul dBTP (C+G+T) 10mM 1 ul Biotin-dATP 1mM 10 ul T4 PNK 1ul Fill up to 100ul with H2O Note: dBTP means al nucleotides except dATP.

Put the samples one by one on the magnet, remove the liquid, and take the tubes off the magnet. Pour the mixture prepared in the previous step on the beads. Gently swirl the beads to resuspend the beads. Be careful not to dry the beads. samples should be treated one by one to avoid drying the beads.
Incubate at 37°C in a thermomixer with agitation at 700 RPM for 30 min.

Put the samples one by one on the magnet, after 1min remove the liquid, and take the tubes off the magnet. Resuspend the beads in 100ul of TdT buffer 1x. Do not vortex the beads. Gently swirl the beads to resuspend the beads
Put the samples one by one on the magnet, remove the liquid, and take the tubes off the magnet. Resuspend the beads in the mixture prepared in step 55. Gently swirl the beads to resuspend the beads.
To each tube add : TdT 4ul Optional: RNase A 1ul TdT is a quite specific enzyme for DNA labelling, as evident by TUNEL assay. Adding RNase A reassures specific DNA labelling.
Incubate at 37°C in a thermomixer with agitation at 800 RPM for 30min.

Wash the beads 4 times with 1ml ice-cold IP buffer at room temp. During each wash, invert the tubes to resuspend the beads. Then spin them briefly. Put them on the magnet, and remove the liquid. Take the tube off the magnet, and pour 1ml wash buffer. Treat the tubes one by one on the magent to avoid drying the beads.
Remove the last IP wash buffer
Resuspend the beads in 100ul, SDS 7.5% + DTT 200mM, and vortex vigorously
Incubate 15min at 37°C in a thermomixer, with 1000 RPM
Put the samples on the magnet, collect the liquid, and discard the beads

Prepare the following mixture for each tube. You may prepare a master mix for all tubes: TdT buffer (including CoCl2) 20ul Biotin-dCTP 5ul Biotin-ddUTP 5ul Fill up to 95ul with H2O Note: Biotin-dCTP was used to reduce the cost. You may use 10ul of biotin-ddUTP.
Dilute the liquid with 1000ul IP buffer
Add 70ul of Streptavidin magnetic beads to each tube. Rotate the tubes for 30-60min at room temptrature.
Put the tubes on the magnet after 2 min discard the solution.

Wash the beads 3 times by SDS wash buffer Each washing consists of resuspending the beads in the washing solution by vortexing, briefly spinning, and putting on the magnet for 2 min to remove the solution After the second wash, normally you should be able to see the beads are dispersed, except the negative control.
Wash with BW2x buffer, once
Wash the beads with Iso-propanol wash buffer, once
Wash the beads with acetonitrile wash buffer, twice
Resuspend the beads in 80ul acetonitrile wash buffer, and transfer it into a PCR tubes. Make sure that all the beads have been transferred
Put the tubes on the magnet, and remove the wash buffer.
Spin the tubes briefly, and remove the residues of the wash buffer
Resuspend the beads in 20ul Ambic 50mM + 10mM DTT (or TEAB 50mM for dimethylation or TMT labelling) Note: freshly prepared Ambic should be used.
Incubate the beads at 50°C for 15min
Chill the beads, and add 2ul of IAA 0.4M IAA: Iodoacetimide
Vortex, and keep the tubes in a drawer for 15-30min
Add 0.5ul of DTT 1M to neutralize IAA
Add 300ng of LysC (3ul of 100ng/ul), vortex, and spin briefly
Incubate overnight at 37 °C (12-16h)
Remove the beads, and transfer the liquid into new PCR tubes. Do not discard the beads as they contain the DNA. Follow the DNA purification steps to prepare the DNA for qPCR or sequencing.
Heat the samples (without the beads) at 95 °C for 5min to reverse the formaldehyde crosslinking
Chill the samples briefly
Add 100ng of Trypsin (1ul of 100ng/ul)
Incubate at 37°C for 8h.
Add 1ul TFA 10% to the digested samples to acidify the pH
Pour 50ul of Acetonitrile 80% + formic acid 0.1% in a glass insert (tubes for auto-sampler)
Set a P20 on 20ul, and pick up a zip-tip
Pipete 20ul Acetonitrile 100%, and discard the liquid. Repeat once again.
Equilibrate the zip-tip by pipetting 20ul TFA 0.1%, and discard the liquid. Repeat twice again.
Pipet one sample 10 times. Do not generate bubbles.
Wash the zip-tip by pipetting 20ul TFA 0.1%, and discard the liquid. Repeat once again. The aliqoute of TFA 0.1% should be discarded to avoid carry over between the samples.
Elute the peptides by pipetting Acetonitrile 80% + formic acid 0.1% in the glass insert
Dry out the eluent in an appropriate speed vac.
Reconstitute the peptides in 8ul DMSO 2% + formic acid 0.1%
Inject 7 out of 8 ul of the samples to the mass spec
Following step 81, resuspend the beads in 23ul of SDS wash buffer
Add 2ul of Proteinase K (20mg/ul)
Incubate the beads at 55 °C for 15 min
Incubate the beads at 95 °C for 10min
Put the tubes on a magnetic rack, and remove the beads.
Collect the liquid, and add 50ul of Ampure XP beads to the samples. Vortex, and wait 10min.
Spin the samples, and put them on a magnetic rack to separate the beads.
discard the liquid, and add 200ul of freshly prepared EtOH 80%, and wait a few second
10-discard the liquid, repeat the previous step once again.
Spin the samples, and put them on a magnetic rack to separate the beads.
Discard the residual ethanol.
Resuspend the beads in 20ul of Tris-Cl 10mM pH 8.0
Spin the samples, and put them on a magnetic rack to separate the beads.
Collect the samples, and transfer them to new tubes. The samples can be used for qPCR or library preparation.
Pour 5ml of streptavidin beads (e.g. NEB S1420S) into a 15mL tube
Transfer the tube on the magnet, wait 5min and then discard the liquid
Wash the beads once again with 10mL of PBS-T PBS-T : PBS + Tween20 0.1% (v/v)
Put the tube on the magnet, wait 5min and then discard the liquid From here work in a fume hood
Resuspend the beads in 7ml of 0.2M Sodium cyanoborohydride in PBS-T. In a fume hood, weight 0.125 g Sodium cyanoborohydride. Then dissolve it in 10ml PBS-T
Add 7ml of Formaldehyde 4% In a fume hood, add 2.5ml formaldehyde 16% to 7.5 ml PBS-T
Rotate 2 hours at room temperature
Put the tube on the magnet, wait 5min and then discard the liquid
Wash the beads with 10ml Tris.Cl 0.1M pH 7.5
Put the tube on the magnet, wait 5min and then discard the liquid
Wash the beads with 10ml PBS-T
Put the tube on the magnet, wait 5min and then discard the liquid
Resuspend the beads in 5mL PBS-T You may wash the original bottle of the beads, and transfer the modified beads to the original bottle.
14-Keep the beads in the fridge. The beads are stable at +4⁰C for months
creator: Sina Rafiee
